alerts.headline: Special Weather Statement issued March 6 at 9:32PM EST by NWS Boston/Norton MA
alerts.description:
Areas of drizzle with pockets of freezing drizzle will continue
into early Saturday morning. The bulk of the freezing drizzle will
be in the high terrain where temperatures were at or below
freezing and untreated roads maybe icy. However...even some of
the lower elevations had temperatures around 32 degrees which may
lead to some patchy black ice.

Plan on allowing extra travel time and be especially careful on
bridges and overpasses. This is particularly true in the high
terrain where temperatures were a few degrees below freezing.
Pedestrians should also be careful in parking lots and walkways
which may have patches of black ice.

Temperatures will warm above freezing by mid to late Saturday
morning alleviating any issues.

alerts.headline: Special Weather Statement issued March 6 at 12:28PM EST by NWS Boston/Norton MA
alerts.description:
Spotty freezing drizzle is possible through tonight north of the
Mass Pike and in the higher elevations. Be alert for icy spots on
untreated roads.

Across the rest of southern New England, including areas near the
coast, temperatures will fall to around freezing later tonight
into early Saturday morning. Leftover moisture may produce icy
spots on untreated roads in these areas as well.

Plan on allowing extra travel time and be especially careful on
bridges and overpasses.

Temperatures will warm above freezing Saturday morning.

nws_forecast.name: Overnight
nws_forecast.detailed: Areas of drizzle and patchy fog. Cloudy, with a low around 34. East wind around 3 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Saturday
nws_forecast.detailed: Areas of drizzle and patchy fog before noon. Cloudy, with a high near 47. South wind 5 to 12 mph.

nws_forecast.name: Saturday Night
nws_forecast.short_forecast: Patchy Fog then Chance Light Rain
nws_forecast.detailed: Patchy fog between 7pm and 8pm, then patchy fog and a slight chance of rain between 8pm and 11pm, then a chance of rain and patchy fog. Mostly cloudy. Low around 41, with temperatures rising to around 47 overnight. Southwest wind around 12 mph, with gusts as high as 25 mph. Chance of precipitation is 50%.

nws_forecast.name: Sunday
nws_forecast.short_forecast: Chance Light Rain then Patchy Fog
nws_forecast.detailed: A chance of rain and patchy fog before 7am, then patchy fog and a slight chance of rain between 7am and 3pm. Mostly cloudy, with a high near 55. Southwest wind around 10 mph, with gusts as high as 22 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
